def: s.m. Hunting, the chase.
= اچيت aćyut, q.v.

adj. Destitute of consciousness; unconscious; inanimate; insensible; senseless; fainting; out of one's mind; thoughtless; heedless, careless, regardless; inattentive; off one's guard; negligent; forgetful; stupid
subs: v.n. To be thoughtless, be insensible (to); to become or be insensible, or in a swoon, &c.

def: adj. = اچيت aćet; and = اچيتنيه aćaitanya, qq.v.
s.f. = H اچيتپنا अचेतपना aćetpanā, s.m. Unconsciousness, insensibility; thoughtlessness, obliviousness; inattention, neglect, forgetfulness; stupidity.
s.m. = H اچيتتا अचेतता aćetatā, s.f. Unconsciousness, insensibility; thoughtlessness, obliviousness; inattention, neglect, forgetfulness; stupidity.
s.f. = اچکري aćkarī, q.v.
adj. Not strained; impure.
s.m. The state of a fallen king; friendlessness, forlorn condition, destitution.
s.f. A complete army, consisting of 109,350 foot, 65,610 horse, 21,870 chariots, and 21,870 elephants.
s.m. Throwing away; convulsion; a putting upon; reproof, censure, blame; sarcasm, irony.
s.m. Ulcers in the throat, the thrush. (Cf. چهارو ćhārū.)
n. To be, exist; to remain, abide, stay.
n. To be, exist; to remain, abide, stay.
s.m. Calumnious accusation; calumny.
s.f. The music of a stringed instrument played so as to express the words of a song. (See next.)
s.f. Orthography; — a inode of playing upon a stringed instrument so as to express the words of a song; — a Turkish guitar, lute or mandoline.
v.n. To repent, regret; to grieve, repine.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Not to be touched, intangible (as food for religious persons, &c.); inviolable; — untouched, untasted; unpolluted, undefiled; unblemished; clean, pure; unused, new.
s.f. A caudle or gruel given to puerperal women. (See ajwāʼinī).
adj. Unpolished, rough.
adj. Undistinguished; without note or consequence.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Thoughtless, regardless, heedless, careless, rash; — a thoughtless person, &c.
s.f. Thoughtlessness, heedlessness, inconsiderateness, disregard, carelessness, unconcern; negligence, neglect.
s.f. Thoughtlessness, heedlessness, inconsiderateness, disregard, carelessness, unconcern; negligence, neglect.
part. adj. Not thought of; unexpected; disregarded.
adj. Not of long duration, brief; transitory, evanescent; not of long date, recent.
adj. Unwished for, undesired.
adj. Not variegated; not painted (paper, &c.).
s.m. A long coat buttoned in front.
s.f. Wantonness, lasciviousness; wickedness.
s.m. = اچرا aćrā, q.v.
v.t. To rinse the mouth, &c. See āćaman.
s.f. Not stealing.

adj. Restless, uneasy, agitated, impatient; unsteady, wavering, irresolute; variable, inconstant, fickle, wanton, giddy, skittish; active, swift, brisk; sprightly, playful, frisky, vivacious; inconsiderate, careless; unruly, refractory, restive, headstrong, self-willed, obstinate, perverse, wayward.

adj. Restless, uneasy, agitated, impatient; unsteady, wavering, irresolute; variable, inconstant, fickle, wanton, giddy, skittish; active, swift, brisk; sprightly, playful, frisky, vivacious; inconsiderate, careless; unruly, refractory, restive, headstrong, self-willed, obstinate, perverse, wayward.

r: aćpalāhaṭ, haṭ, aćpalāpan, pan, aćpaltā, ćapalatā, aćpalī, aćpalā
def: (-haṭ = S. त्व), s.f. = H اچپلاپن अचपलापन aćpalāpan (-pan = S. त्वं), s.m. = H اچپلتا अचपलता aćpaltā (S. चपलता ćapalatā), s.f. = H اچپلي अचपली aćpalī, s.f. Restlessness; fickleness, inconstancy; playfulness, &c. (See aćpalā.)

r: aćpalāpan, pan, aćpalāhaṭ, haṭ, aćpaltā, ćapalatā, aćpalī, aćpalā
def: (-pan = S. त्वं), s.m. = H اچپلاهٿ अचपलाहट aćpalāhaṭ (-haṭ = S. त्व), s.f. = H اچپلتا अचपलता aćpaltā (S. चपलता ćapalatā), s.f. = H اچپلي अचपली aćpalī, s.f. Restlessness; fickleness, inconstancy; playfulness, &c. (See aćpalā.)

subs: v.n. To be possessed with a feeling of wonder, to be astonished
To do something wonderful; to perform a miracle.

subs: v.n. To be possessed with a feeling of wonder, to be astonished
To do something wonderful; to perform a miracle.

= اچهتانا پچهتانا aćhtānā-paćhtānā, q.v.
adj. Unfailing, sure, unerring; infallible; certain
adj. Lit. '(A woman) inducing men to come near her'; weak, foolish, stupid, senseless, idiotic.

s.f. = اچهواني aćhwānī, and اجوائني ajwāʼinī, qq.v.

def: s.m. (f. ?) & adj. Beginning, commencement, starting-point; original; — first, prior, primary; primeval; pre-eminent; (often at the end of a compound in the sense) beginning with, et cetera, and so forth, and so on, onwards, &c. (e.g. manushyādi jantu, 'man and the other animals'; ek-ād [or -ādh], one or so)
subs: ādyant, s.m. The first and the last; the beginning and the end; — adv. From first to last; from beginning to end; from head to foot, throughout; without cessation or intermission, incessantly, always
s.m. The Old Testament (opposed to anta-bhāg)
s.m. The first male or progenitor (usually applied to Vishnu); the primeval spirit
From the beginning; and so forth

def: s.f. Grace, beauty; elegance; graceful manner on carriage; charm, fascination; blandishment; amorous signs and gestures, coquetry
subs: adj. Understanding signs and gestures, skilled in the arts of blandishment
s.f. Understanding of the arts of blandishment, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ادا (adā), from Arabic أَدَاء (ʔadāʔ).

def: s.f. The act of bringing to completion, &c.; completion, perfection; performance; fulfilment; accomplishment; acquittance; payment or discharge (of a debt, &c.)
subs: s.f. Fixing a period for the performance of a contract, or for the payment of a debt; liquidation of a debt by instalments ( = qist-bandī)
Performance of service, discharge of duty
v.t. To perform; accomplish; fulfil; discharge; liquidate, pay; to effect or accomplish satisfactorily, properly, &c.

v.n. To be performed, accomplished, fulfilled, discharged; to be paid, liquidated; to be done, effected, or accomplished satisfactorily or properly, &c.; to be brought out, uttered or pronounced distinctly (a letter or word in reading); to be done for, undone, demolished, made an end of.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ادا (adā), from Arabic أَدَاء (ʔadāʔ).

def: s.m. Ginger in the undried state. (See adrak.)
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit आर्द्रक (ārdraka), through Prakrit *𑀆𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀅 (*āddaa), from आर्द्र (ārdra, “wet”), as fresh ginger is wet. Doublet of اَدْرَک (adrak). Cognates include Bengali আদা (ada), Gujarati આદુ (ādu), Nepali अदुवा (aduwā).

def: s.m. pl. (of عدو ʻadū), Enemies, foes.

s.m. (pl. of ادب adab), and sing. ( = adab); Good breeding; good manners; politeness; polite accomplishments; elegance of manners, address or speech, &c.; rules; ceremonies; etiquette; forms of address in writing and speaking; salutation; respects; devoirs; — interj. Good morning! good day! &c.; my compliments to you! thank you! your humble servant!
subs: ādāb bajā-lānā, v.n. To pay (one's) respects; to salute respectfully, make a salām, make obeisance; to use complimentary expressions; to bow deferentially, to acknowledge (one) as superior; to say good-bye, to take leave
Best, or most humble, compliments; best respects
ādāb-ě-shukrīya, The set form or established etiquette for making one's acknowledgments; making a formal return of thanks for favours received
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آدَاب (ādāb), from Arabic آدَاب (ʔādāb).

def: s.m. Discipline, training; deportment; good breeding; good manners, politeness, courtesy, urbanity; etiquette; polite literature
subs: (-ko), To teach manners; to discipline; chastise
(-kā), To show deference or respect (to), treat with respect; to respect, revere
s.m. A polite or well-bred person; one who ought to be treated with politeness or respect.
ex: اُن کا بیٹا ہمیشہ ادب سے بزرگوں سے بات کرتا ہے۔
unkā beṭā hameśā adab se buzurgõ se bāt kartā hai.
Their son always talks to elders with politeness.

def: s.m. A number, a whole number; a figure; a coefficient; one (of anything)
subs: The first figure; the first term (of a series); a prime number
A whole number, an integer; — a unit

def: s.m. Incommensurable numbers, prime numbers
adj. Numeral, numerical.
s.m. Covering; coverlet; cover; wrapper. (Cf. H. ḍhaknā; S. sthagana.)
adj. (compar. & super. of دقيق daqīq), More, most, or very subtile, nice, abstruse, recondite or obscure.
adj. Leafless;- s.m. A plant, Eugenia (or Barringtonia) acutangulā.

ety: for A. عدالة, inf. n. of عدل 'to act equitably,' &c.
def: s.f. Equity, justice, law; — a court of law or justice, assize, tribunal
subs: or ʻadālat-ě-ḵẖurd, s.f. A small-cause court
s.f. A court of civil jurisdiction, civil court
s.f. A court of criminal jurisdiction, criminal court
v.n. To administer or dispense justice; to exercise judicial powers, to judge, to try suits at law
adv. By law, judicially; by a power of the court
s.f. A court of competent jurisdiction, a competent court or tribunal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَدَالَت (adālat), from Arabic عَدَالَة (ʕadāla).

def: s.f. The two courts of law, i.e. the civil and the criminal.
adj. Equitable, just, right, true; legal, judicial; actionable.
v.t. To exchange, barter; to change; convert; trasmute; transfer; shift; displace; confuse; transform; pervert.
s.m. Adam, the first man; man (as in comp.)
subs: s.m. Lit. 'Having eyes like those of a man'; a horse, the whites of whose eyes have a red margin
s.m. Lit. 'Born of Adam'; the human race, man, mankind
adj. Humane; benevolent; — ādam-garī, s.f. Humanity; benevolence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آدَم (ādam), borrowed from Arabic آدَم (ʔādam), borrowed from Biblical Hebrew אָדָם (āḏā́m), from Proto-Semitic *ʔadam-.

ety: inf. n. of عدم 'to be destitute of, to lack,' &c.
def: s.m. Non-possession, lack, want; non-existence, nonentity; nothing; annihilation; state of being lost; loss (of); absence; paucity, deficiency; default (of); neediness, destitution; non-performance, non-observance

s.f. Insufficiency or want of means; no assets; insolvency; impecuniosity; inability to pay; lack of power; disability, &c.
s.m. Non-existence of a promise; non assumpsit
s.m. Non-entry (in most of the numerous compounds of this description ʻadam may generally be rendered by non-, or 'want of,' or 'absence of,' &c.)
adj. lit. 'Like the non-existence'; non-existent; null and void
(-ko), To regard as non-existent; to treat as null and void; to set at nought
v.t. To make null and void, to annul, rescind, &c.
v.n. To become extinct; to come to nought; to come to an end; to be rescinded, &c.

def: May God preserve (him, &c.).
s.m. The sounding of trumpets; charge sounded by musical instruments; the trumpeting of elephants; noise and bustle; arrogance, pride, ostentation.
adj. (f. -ī), Breathless, dead.
s.m. Taking, receiving, acceptance
s.m. Act of eating; food; nourishment.
adj. Free from strife; quiet, tranquil.
adj. Free from punishment; unpunished; — s.m. Impunity
adj. & s.m. = اداتا adātā), q.v.

ety: pl. of ادني adnā, mistakenly, for ادنا
def: adj. & s.m. The lowest, meanest, vilest, most ignoble or contemptible.
adj. = ادتري adatrī, q.v.
adj. = ادتري adatrī, q.v.

v.n. = ādar pānā, q.v: ādar-yogya, adj. Worthy of respect, esteem, or reverence; respectable, estimable, venerable.

v.t. To respect, &c. = ādar karnā, q.v.s.v. ādar.
s.m. A mirror; commentary; an original manuscript from which a copy is taken; copy; specimen; sign.
adj. Unseen; unforeseen; — s.m. Unseen danger; fate, fortune; — a mirror ( = ādarś).
s.m. A kind of very fine muslin; a kind of sweetmeat.
s.m. A mirror.
adj. & s.m. Invisible, unseen; — disappearance, invisibility.
s.m. Making apparent, showing; a mirror.
s.m. A ditch and the mound of earth thrown up from it, forming the boundary of a field (cf. āḍ).
adj. Respectful; reverential; devout; respectable, estimable; to be revered; — s.m. (f. -vatī), A devout person, &c.
s.f. Lentils.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عدس ('adas), from Arabic عَدَس (ʕadas).

def: s.f. Instrument, tool, implement; utensil; apparatus; — (in Gram.) a particle.

ety: for A. عادة, v.n. fr. عود 'to return' (to
def: s.f. Custom, habit, manner, wont, usage, practice

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عادت ('ādat), from Arabic عَادَة (ʕāda).

def: adj. & s.m. Niggardly, illiberal, stingy, avaricious; — niggard, miser.

ety: for A. عادةً, acc. of ʻādat
def: adv. From habit, habitually.
adj. Customary, usual, habitual; — s.m. (met.) A catamite.
adj. Hanging, pendulous, suspended (in or between); infirm. (Cf. the form adhantrī.)
s.m. A donation which is null and void.
part. adj. & s.f. Not given in marriage; an unmarried girl.

s.f. (See next.)

subs: (-se), To bear enmity (to), to bear malice (towards)

adj. Inimical, hostile; malicious; felonious.
adj. (f. -ā), Merciless, unkind, unfeeling, cruel.
v.n. To be in a flutter, be agitated, uneasy; to be perplexed; to waver; to be powerless; to be active or brisk

ety: S. अट्ट+क; and cf. S. हट्ट, H. हाट
def: s.m. The shed or place in which kahārs, palanquin bearers, coolies, carters, &c. assemble or abide; stand, station, assembly-room; lounge or meeting-place of idlers or pleasure-seekers; — a square frame for embroidery; perch of a bird-cage; heel of a shoe; one of the (usually) two divisions of land watered by a well (see āḍ); — a fixed measure or standard
subs: s.m. The keeper or owner of an aḍḍā.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Half; — s.m. A half-measure; a half-chain, half-yard, &c.

(S. ardha+ śirsā), s.f. Pain affecting half the head, hemicrania ( = adh-kapālī)
To be or become half; to be reduced to half the former size, &c., to grow thin, become emaciated
adv. Half and half; half on one side and half on the other; equally divided; in two (equal parts); bisected
v.t. To cut in two, to halve, bisect; to divide or share equally.

def: s.f. Half a damṛī, or half a pāʼī; half-piece (thān) of cloth.
adj. Worth one addhī, or half a pāʼī; s.m. Anything worth half a pāʼī.

s.m. Order, command, injunction; instruction; prediction; a form of salutation among jogīs and other religious mendicants to a superior, good day, good morrow; good-bye, farewell; (in Gram.) the substitution of one letter or syllable for another.
s.m. Procrastination, evasion, shuffling

adj. Improper or unfit to be given.

s.m. Half an anna, a copper coin worth two pice

subs: adv. Half and half, &c. = ādho-ādh, q.v.

To be or become half; to be reduced to half the former size, &c., to grow thin, become emaciated

def: s.f. = S ادهيريه अधैर्य adhairya, adj. = ادهيرج adhīraj, q.v.
adj. = S ادهيريا अधैर्या adhairyā, s.f. = ادهيرج adhīraj, q.v.
s.m. Half; part. (Rare.)
s.m. A measure of capacity for dry measure (about 750 cub. in. English measure).
s.m. A black horse, a dark-coloured horse.
adj. Mean, vile, contemptible, very lowest; inferior, low, humble, wretched; — s.m. A low person; a vile wretch; meanest or most unworthy person
subs: adj. The vilest of the vile, exceedingly vile
s.m. An epithet of the Deity (as He who affords support to the lowest and most unworthy).

def: s.m. One who is in reduced circumstances through debt; a debtor.
s.f. Vileness, contemptibleness, meanness, lowness, inferiority, unworthiness, wretchedness.
s.m. Pregnancy, conception; a ceremony performed previous to conception as conducive to it; — deposit, surety, pledge.
s.m. Water set to boil, or which is boiling, for cooking food.
adj. Destitute of wealth or property; poor, needy, destitute; — s.m. A pauper.
s.m. Oils, unguents. (Prop. in H. अद्हान ad-hān).
s.m. One of the thirty-six Rāginīs that are played or sung during the night only. (Also written aṛhānā and aṛānā).
adj. = ادهن adhan, q.v.
s.m. A ceremony performed after cohabitation to procure or favour conception.
adj. & adv. Without prop, support or fastening; unsupported; dangling, pendulous, suspended; not fixed or firm, loose; separate, detached; (cf. next)

s.m. The lower lip, the lip; the lower region or part; pudendum muliebre; the space between earth and sky, mid-air; empty space; middle; intervening space; interval; interim; — adj. Inferior, low, insignificant, vile; — adv. In mid-air, in the middle or midst, &c. (see adhar-meṅ); stiltedly; on tip-toe
subs: (adhara+amrita), s.m. Nectar of the lips; moisture of the lips
v.t. To raise or lift up in the air, to raise from the ground, raise on high
s.m.f. One whose mind is engrossed with sublunary matters; a person of inferior or superficial understanding
v.n. To walk stiffly, stiltedly, or affectedly; to walk on tiptoe
adv. In the middle, in the midst (of); midway; between; in the interval.

def: s.m. Support, prop, stay; stand, pedestal, base (of a geom. fig., &c., syn. bhūmi and neʼo); supporter, patron; subsistence, nourishment, food, aliment; meal; — dike, canal; basin round the foot of a tree; comprehension; location.
s.m. A bullock that has not borne a burden, or has not been broken in for work.
adj. & s.m. Eating; — eater (used in comp. e.g. dūdh-ādhārī, s.m. One who lives on milk).
s.m. Unrighteousness, irreligion, impiety; wickedness, iniquity, guilt, sin, crime, immorality, wrong-doing; injustice; — adj. Unrighteous, irreligious, impious, iniquitous, wicked, wrong, unjust
subs: adj. & s.m. Practising wickedness, &c.; — an impious person, &c.

def: s.m., = ادهرم adharm, q.v.

adj. Unrighteous, irreligious, impious; immoral, iniquitous, wicked, sinful, criminal; wrong, unlawful, unjust; heartless, cruel; deprived of caste; — s.m. An irreligious or wicked person; criminal, &c.
adj. Unjust, unrighteous, wicked.
adj. Unjust, unrighteous, wicked.
s.m. Unrighteousness, sinfulness; impiousness, immorality, wickedness, unjustness, lawlessness, &c.
adj. & s.m. Most wicked, very impious; — a very wicked person.
s.m., = S ادهرمتا अधर्मता adharmatā, s.f. = ادهرم adharm, q.v.
v.t. To rest upon, rely on, trust in.
= ادهيرپنا adherpanā, q.v.
s.m. A half-piece of cloth; a sort of cloth (cf. adarsā).
= ādhā-sīsī, q.v.s.v. آدها ādhā.
adj. = ادتري adatrī, q.v.
s.f. A half-hide (of a bull, buffalo, &c.); the coarsest and thickest sort of leather, such as is used for the soles of shoes, buckets, traces, &c.; — hide; maw; stomach
subs: or tan-jānā, v.n. to be distended — the skin or hide; the stomach to be distended; to be gorged or stuffed.

def: Half a pice (paisā), a small copper coin, equal to four damṛīs.
s.f. Half-rupee; half-gold mohur; half-share.
s.m. Holder or proprietor of a half-share ( = adhiyā); — anything worth half a pice (adhelā).
adj. Middle-aged; just past the prime of life.
s.m. The middle period of life, manhood, mature age.
prep. (used in comp.), Over, above, upon, over and above. (It implies superiority in point of place, quantity, or quality, and is the reverse of apa).
s.m. Care, anxious reflection, mental agony, anxiety; expectation; calamity; pawn, pledge; engagement; site, location.
s.m. A prosiding or tutelary deity; the supreme deity.
part. Obtained, acquired; gone over, conned, studied, learned.

adj. Additional, extra; augmented; exceeding, surpassing, excelling; excessive; abundant; superior, greater; very great; more numerous; much; more; most; — adv. & postpos. Exceedingly; abundantly; excessively; greatly; very; in excess (of); too much; too great; over and above; in addition (to); besides
subs: (-se), To be more (than); to be in excess (of); to exceed, be over, &c.

def: s.f. = S ادهکتا अधिकता adhiktā, s.f. Increase, addition, augmentation; abundance; excess; surplus; greatness, superiority, supremacy; aggrandizement; oppression, violence.
v.t. To augment, increase.
s.m. Authority, power; government, rule, administration; lordship, sovereignty; office, appointment; possession, proprietorship; inheritance; right, title, privilege
subs: To give authority (to), to authorize; to invest with power or authority; to confer a right; to appoint
To have authority, have a right or claim; to hold an office or appointment
To exercise authority, to administer, govern, rule, control, &c.
To obtain the office or appointment (of), to be invested with power or authority, be empowered; to obtain the title, &c. (of).

def: s.m. Superiority; title, right; (in Gram.) the locative case; an adverb.

s.m. Governor, ruler, minister; functionary, official, officer, (and generally) one invested with power or authority; proprietor, owner, master; heir; agent; deputy; one who possesses a right, title, or privilege.

ety: abst. s. from adhikār & = S. adhikāritā
def: s.f. The state of holding office; governorship; proprietorship, ownership, &c.
s.m. Superintendent; inspector of receipts and disbursements, auditor of public accounts.
s.f. = H ادهکائي अधिकाई adhikāʼī, s.f. Increase, addition, augmentation; abundance; excess; surplus; greatness, superiority, supremacy; aggrandizement; oppression, violence.
s.m. Excess, overplus, abundance.
s.m. Proud flesh or cancer, especially in the eyes or the back part of the gums.
s.m. An intercalary month formed of the aggregate of the days omitted in reckoning the lunar year.
postpos. (gov. gen. with -ke), Subject, under the authority (of), subservient, dependent, subordinate; submissive, obedient; obsequious; docile, tractable, humble, meek, lowly
subs: (-ke), To remain subject, or in allegiance (to)
(-ke), To be subject (to); to submit (to); to obey; to be humble.

def: postpos. (gov. gen. with -ke), Subject, under the authority (of), subservient, dependent, subordinate; submissive, obedient; obsequious; docile, tractable, humble, meek, lowly
subs: (-ke), To remain subject, or in allegiance (to)
(-ke), To be subject (to); to submit (to); to obey; to be humble.

def: s.f. Subjection, subservience, dependence, subordination, obedience, submissiveness, humility, meekness; obsequiousness, servility
subs: To practise humility; to beg, beseech, pray, entreat, petition.

def: s.f. Subjection, subservience, dependence, subordination, obedience, submissiveness, humility, meekness; obsequiousness, servility
subs: To practise humility; to beg, beseech, pray, entreat, petition.

def: s.f. Subjection, subservience, dependence, subordination, obedience, submissiveness, humility, meekness; obsequiousness, servility
subs: To practise humility; to beg, beseech, pray, entreat, petition.

def: s.f. Subjection, subservience, dependence, subordination, obedience, submissiveness, humility, meekness; obsequiousness, servility
subs: To practise humility; to beg, beseech, pray, entreat, petition.

def: s.m. Owner, lord, master; chief, commander; king, prince.
s.m. A superintendent or an inspector of schools.
s.m. = ادهپ adhipa, q.v.
s.m. Supremacy, sovereignty; lordship, governorship.
adj. Unsteady, unstable, &c., see ادهيرج adhīraj.
s.m. A great or supreme king, emperor, monarch, potentate.
adj. Unsteady; irresolute; spiritless; fickle, vacillating; impatient, restless; hasty, precipitate; confused, perplexed, distracted
s.m. Supreme sway, empire.
adj. (f. -ī) = ادهيرج adhīraj, q.v.
s.f. Unsteadiness, want of firmness; fickleness; impatience; haste, precipitation; perplexity, confusion.
s.m. Master, lord, chief.
s.m. Residence, abode, seat.
s.m. Protector, ruler, governor.
s.m. Supreme lord or king, emperor.
part. Studied, read, learned.
adj. Well-read, proficient; sound; firm; good.
s.m. House, dwelling, residence; — perfuming the person; perfumes.
s.m. Dowry or alimony given to a wife who is superseded by a second marriage.
s.f. A superseded wife.

s.f. Division into two equal parts or shares; division of produce between two parties in equal shares, one furnishing the land, seed, &c., and the other the labour; the half produce which a zamīndār takes from a tenant in lieu of money rent; the payment of half the annual assessment by the cultivators to the Government agent for the revenue at the spring and autumnal harvests
v.t. To halve; to divide.
adv. Below, down, downward; beneath, under
subs: s.f. Downward movement, descent; degradation; hell
adj. & adv. Having the face or head downwards; hanging down the head (through grief or shame, &c.); headlong; upside down, inverted; topsy-turvy.

def: s.f. A fine kind of cloth.
adj. Not fixed; uncertain, doubtful.
adv. At this time, now.
adj. New, recent, of the present moment, or time.
s.f. Newness, recentness, the being modern; novelty.
adj. Half-formed; half-done, half-ready or -completed; unfinished, incomplete; immature (as a fœtus, &c.); weak, feeble; incapable
subs: v.n. To remain unfinished, imperfect, incomplete, &c.
v.t. To render weak, helpless, useless, &c., to enfeeble.

def: s.m. Road, &c. = adhvan, q.v.
adj. Middle-aged.
s.m. Road, way; course.
s.m. Half-measure, half-maund; half of anything.
s.m. A traveller.
v.s.v. ادهہ adh.

s.f. Division into two equal parts or shares; division of produce between two parties in equal shares, one furnishing the land, seed, &c., and the other the labour; the half produce which a zamīndār takes from a tenant in lieu of money rent; the payment of half the annual assessment by the cultivators to the Government agent for the revenue at the spring and autumnal harvests
adj. Rich in, abounding in; possessing in abundance.
= ادهيکش adhyaksh, q.v.
s.f. Property or settlement given to a wife when superseded by a new marriage of the husband: it is given during the marriage ceremony over the nuptial fire.
part. Supplied; argued; understood; scire licet.
s.m. One who exercises supervision; inspector, superintendent; master, lord, ruler.
adv. On the subject of syllables; above all syllables, the mystic Om.
s.f. The position or office of adhyaksh, supervisorship, inspectorship, &c.
corr. from ادهيين adhyayan, q.v.
s.m. One who instructs in the sacred books, spiritual preceptor; teacher; a learned man.
s.m. Teaching the sacred books; instructing.

s.m. Holder of a half share; a man who holds and cultivates land in two villages, and spends half his time in one and half in the other; a cultivator who receives half the crop for his assistance in cultivating land.
s.f. Half-share.
s.m. Act of mounting or climbing; act of raising.
s.m. Act of mounting or climbing; act of raising.
s.m. Erroneously attributing; error, mistake.
part. Seated upon.
s.m. The supreme soul.
s.m. Section; book; chapter; article; lesson.
s.m. Reading, studying (especially the Vedas); study, application.
s.m. Solicitation, entreaty.
s.m. Student, scholar, pupil, disciple.
part. Inhabited, peopled.

s.m. (f. ?) & adj. Beginning, commencement, starting-point; original; — first, prior, primary; primeval; pre-eminent; (often at the end of a compound in the sense) beginning with, et cetera, and so forth, and so on, onwards, &c. (e.g. manushyādi jantu, 'man and the other animals'; ek-ād [or -ādh], one or so)
subs: ādyant, s.m. The first and the last; the beginning and the end; — adv. From first to last; from beginning to end; from head to foot, throughout; without cessation or intermission, incessantly, always
s.m. The Old Testament (opposed to anta-bhāg)
s.m. The first male or progenitor (usually applied to Vishnu); the primeval spirit
From the beginning; and so forth

def: adj. Accustomed, habituated, addicted (to); habitual, customary, wonted
subs: (-kā), To be accustomed (to), to be addicted (to).

def: part. adj. & s.m. Passing; passing beyond; transition; superseding; exceeding (a limit); transgressing, acting wrongfully or unjustly, &c.; unjust, wicked; — an unjust man; a transgressor; an enemy.
s.m. A teacher of manners (adab); teacher; — adj. Polite, courteous.

ety: بدي badī, — rt. S. वद् 'to speak,' — redupl.
def: s.f. Predestination; fate, destiny; — allegation; slander, calumny.

ety: अ neg.+rt. of H. डिगना, q.v.
def: adj. Immovable, firm, steady, not tottering, shaky or ricketty, &c. (See aṭal.)
adj. & s.m. Relating to the first or to the beginning; beginning with; and other, and the rest (used of animate objects).
corr. of ادهك adhik, q.v.

ety: act. part. of عدل 'to act justly,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Equitable, just, right; upright, sincere; — one who acts equitably or justly, a just man; one whose testimony is trustworthy.
adj. & s.m. Equitable, just; — alike, equal; — a distributor of justice; the like, or equal (of a thing; syn. naz̤īr); a counterbalancing load.
s.m. Proofs; indications; arguments; evidences.
s.m. Scented leather (brought from Arabia); surface (of the earth or heavens); (in Pers.) face.
adj. Not having, being without, lacking, destitute (of); deprived (of); not to be found
subs: adj. Without a like or parallel, incomparable.

def: s.m. An inauspicious or unlucky day.
s.m. Friday.
s.m. Adverse fortune, ill-luck, misfortune; fate, destiny.
adv. From the beginning; et cetera; and the like; first.
= آدك ādik, q.v.
= آدتيه āditya, q.v.
s.f. The mother of the gods
subs: s.m. A son of Aditi; an epithet of any male deity; a divine being.

def: s.m. Priority, precedence.
s.m. A certain class of deities (twelve in number), sons of Aditi; a deity (in general); the sun

ety: inf. n. of عدل 'to act equitably,' &c.
def: s.m. Equity, justice, rectitude
subs: v.n. To do justice, to act equitably, to decide according to equity and rectitude; to judge (between), to decide
s.f. The dispensing, or the administration, of justice.

def: v.t. To exchange, barter; to change; convert; trasmute; transfer; shift; displace; confuse; transform; pervert.
v.t. To exchange, barter; to change; convert; trasmute; transfer; shift; displace; confuse; transform; pervert.
adj. Gluttonous.
s.m. A descendant of Adam; a human being; man; individual, person; adult; a sensible, or honest man; mankind; people; husband; wife; servant, attendant, retainer
subs: v.t. To humanize, civilize; to make a rational creature of; to teach manners
adv. Per man; severally, individually; one by one
or ho-jānā, To become a man, attain to manhood; to become civilized, learn manners, &c.; to recover one's reason; come to one's senses.

def: s.f. Human nature, humanity; benevolence, compassion, sympathy; civility, urbanity, politeness; good breeding; rationality, reason, judgment
subs: To be bereft of (one's) humanity; to lose (one's) reason, be out of one's senses; to lose one's manners
To become humanized or civilized; to acquire good manners or politeness; to recover one's reason, come to one's senses
ādmīyat-meṅ lānā, v.t. To humanize or civilize; to teach manners; to bring to reason, or to one's senses
To practise humanity; to act as a man.

ety: inf. n. of عدن 'to abide, dwell'
def: s.f. Eden, Paradise; — ʻadan, Name of a town in the province of Yaman in the south of Arabia, Aden.

adj. (compar. & super. of دنی danī), Low; lower; lowest; lowly, humble; inferior; mean, base, ignoble, contemptible; insignificant, unimportant, petty, trifling, trivial; small, little, slight; — s.m. A low, common, or obscure person, &c.; common people; the lower orders
subs: s.m. High and low; the highest and lowest.

def: adj. Faultless, sinless, guiltless, blameless; innocent; without reproach, stainless, spotless; pure, irreproachable.
adj. Not shaking or swinging; not ricketty; immovable, firm, steady; unshaken.
adj. Faultless, sinless, guiltless, blameless; innocent; without reproach, stainless, spotless; pure, irreproachable.
adj. Without brilliance or splendour; lustreless.
s.m. The sixth Nakshatra or lunar mansion; the beginning of the rainy season.
s.f. Green ginger (Amomum zinziber).
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat, pieces of ginger cooked in syrup; candied ginger.
s.m. Rock, stone; hill; mountain.
= ادرشيه adr̤iśya, q.v.
part. adj. Unseen, invisible; unforeseen; — s.m. The unseen; fate, fortune; unforeseen danger.
adj. (f. -ā), Unseen, unperceived; invisible.
s.m. Absence of, or freedom from, malice, or tyranny; moderation, mildness.
adj. & s.m. Free from malice, &c.; not tyrannical; moderate; mild; innocuous; — one who is free from malice, &c.; a non-tyrannical person.
s.m. Rough estimate or calculation, &c. (also written ارسٿا arsaṭṭā, and اڙسٿا aṛsaṭṭā, q.v. and cf. saṭṭā).

ety: for A. ʻaduwwuṉ, v.n. fr. عدو 'to transgress,'
def: s.m. An enemy, a foe (syn. dushman; bairī).
adj. Peerless, &c. = adwitīya, q.v.
s.f. Peerlessness, matchlessness; singleness.
adj. (compar. & super. of دون dūn), More, most, or very mean or wretched; most vile; very vile.
s.f. The strings at the foot of a bedstead, by which the cross strings are tightened or braced
subs: To tighten the strings of a bedstead.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi اَدْواݨ /ਅਦਵਾਣ (advāṇ), from Sanskrit *अन्तदामनी (*antadāmanī).

def: adj. = ادند adand, q.v.
s.m. Revolutions; periods, ages.
s.f. The strings at the foot of a bedstead, by which the cross strings are tightened or braced
subs: To tighten the strings of a bedstead.

def: adj. Not malicious; inoffensive, harmless.
adj. Not malicious; inoffensive, harmless.
adj. Without a second ( = lā - s̤ānī), peerless, matchless, unequalled, unique; only, sole.
adj. Without a second ( = lā - s̤ānī), peerless, matchless, unequalled, unique; only, sole.
s.f. Medicines; remedies.
s.f. pl. of adwiya, q.v.
